opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My cheek is covered in these burning blisters that popped up overnight, and they are spreading right up to my eye. It hurts to even blink.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Painful blisters on my cheek that appeared overnight and are near my eye.

symptom_narrative.character: grouped burning fluid-filled blisters on red skin, some crusting
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: burning, throbbing, very anxious about the eye
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: right cheek just below the eye, about 1.5 cm from the lower lid
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: tingling before the blisters; no vision change yet; no red/painful eye yet

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: recent stress and a lot of sun at the beach before it started

objective_data_offered.value: a 2cm cluster of grouped small blisters on red skin on the right cheek about 1.5cm below the eye; a few have burst with early crusting
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: camera view; cannot do a proper eye (slit-lamp/fluorescein) exam

objective_data_offered.value: vision feels normal, no red/bloodshot eye, no light sensitivity or gritty feeling yet
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-report; ocular involvement cannot be excluded remotely
